titleStmt.title: Letter from the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland to Sir Arthur Stanley, 31 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland (RCPI) to Sir Arthur Stanley (1869-1947) regarding Irish representation on the Council of the new College of Nursing. In the opinion of the RCPI in order to preserve unanimity existing among Irish nurses, and to further the interests of the college, the question of Irish representation on the college council should be settled as quickly as possible.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.placeName: Royal College of Physicians, Kildare Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
31st MAY 16. The Hon.Arthur Stanley, M.V.O.,
85 Pall Mall, London, S.W., Dear Sir, I am directed by the Committee of the Royal Colleges of
Physicians and Surgeons, Ireland, to write to tell you that in
the opinion of the Members of the Committee it is most desirable,
in order to preserve the unanimity at present existing among
Irish Nurses, and so to further the interests of the College of
Nursing, that the question of the Irish representation on the
Council of the College should be settled at as early a date as
possible. The Committee is anxious to call together a meeting of Irish
Nurses and I shall be glad if you can inform me of the date on
which I may expect to have a definite answer on the subject. I remain,
Dear Sir,
Yours faithfully,
Fellow & Registrar, R.C.P.I.
